2606.12160.title: A Controlled Study of Decoding-Time Truthfulness Methods on Instruction-Tuned LLMs
2606.12160.paper_summary: In this work, we introduce CHAIR (Classifier of Hallucination As ImproveR), a supervised framework for detecting hallucinations by analyzing internal logits from each layer of every token. Our method extracts a compact set of features such as maximum, minimum, mean, standard deviation, and slope-from the token logits across all layers, enabling effective hallucination detection without overfitting. Experiments on TruthfulQA and MMLU datasets demonstrate that CHAIR significantly improves detection accuracy, particularly in zero-shot scenarios, showcasing its robustness and generalizability. Beyond hallucination detection, CHAIR highlights the potential of using internal representations for designing advanced decoding strategies. By leveraging patterns in logits, we suggest that more sophisticated models and adaptive decoding methods could further reduce hallucinations and enhance text completion quality. CHAIR not only offers a practical solution for detecting hallucinations but also lays the groundwork for exploring richer representations in LLMs to improve their factuality and coherence.
2606.12160.paper_summary_zh: 在這項工作中，我們介紹了CHAIR（Classifier of Hallucination As ImproveR），這是一個通過分析每個標記每層的內部logits來檢測幻覺的監督框架。<lb/>我們的方法提取了一組緊湊的特徵，如最大值、最小值、平均值、標準差和斜率——來自所有層的標記logits，使得有效的幻覺檢測在不過擬合的情況下得以實現。<lb/>在TruthfulQA和MMLU數據集上的實驗表明，CHAIR顯著提高了檢測準確性，特別是在零樣本場景中，展示了其穩健性和可泛化性。<lb/>除了幻覺檢測，CHAIR還突顯了利用內部表示來設計先進解碼策略的潛力。<lb/>通過利用logits中的模式，我們建議更複雜的模型和自適應解碼方法可以進一步減少幻覺並提高文本完成質量。<lb/>CHAIR不僅提供了一個檢測幻覺的實用解決方案，還為探索更豐富的LLMs表示奠定了基礎，以改善其事實性和連貫性。
2606.12160.author: Ao Sun et.al.
